The statistical model favors Bosnia and Herzegovina at 46%, but assigns them only a modest edge over a competitive underdog. Qatar's win probability stands at 23%, leaving room for either side to emerge with three points on a neutral ground.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 31%, suggesting a closely matched contest where stalemate ranks as a plausible outcome. The gap between a Bosnia win and a Qatar win is substantial in probability terms, yet neither team dominates the expected shape decisively. Draws at this frequency indicate neither favorite carries overwhelming superiority.

The market consensus departs meaningfully from the model, rating Bosnia and Herzegovina at 61%—roughly 15 percentage points higher than the statistical forecast—while correspondingly lowering Qatar to 15%. This divergence is material and reflects materially different assessments of the matchup's competitive balance. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with sportsbook consensus on both Bosnia and Qatar but the dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects actual match dynamics.
